calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-04-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Spalding, via Zoom, for and with Wen Xu, Personal Representative.

The court points to the issue of the request for distribution of the minor beneficiary's interest which should be done to the minor's court-appointed guardian of the estate. Mr. Spalding acknowledges the court's position and offers to delineate the proposed order or submit a revised order to the court. The court instructs counsel to submit a revised order via email to the assigned Examiner. The court points to the issue of the probate referee not likely taking the LLC's accounts into consideration when appraising the Decedent's interest in the LLC. Mr. Spalding offers testimony regarding the LLC issue and advises that he is waiving the statutory fees in the amount of $582.52.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. William Barry. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-26-2022 Continued To Aug-16-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-21-2022 Continued To Jul-26-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Spalding, via Zoom, for Wen Xu, Administrator.

Counsel reports of difficulties formatting an accounting to comply with this court's rules and that he has sent the accounting to an outside source for assistance. Counsel advises the court that an Inventory and Appraisal ("I&A") has been signed and is being returned from China. The court confirms with counsel that the remaining 75% of the shareholders of the LLC need to consent to the transfer of Decedent's 25% membership. Counsel points to a third supplement which is pending in the court's e-filing queue.

The matters are continued to Jul-26-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status on Filing of Second Supplemental Inventory and Appraisal is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Two-Year Accounting
Off Calendar Jun-21-2022 Continued To Jul-26-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar May-24-2022 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-24-2022 Continued To Jun-21-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Spalding, via Zoom, for Xu Wen, Personal Representative.

Mr. Spalding reports that a calendaring oversight caused him to miss the previous calling of this matter. Counsel reports that he is currently working on the accounting and that additional assets have been discovered that were not included in the partial Inventory and Appraisal on file with the court. The court queries counsel whether the other members of the LLC must agree to transfer the decedent's share. Counsel advises that he is uncertain regarding the court's question and will need to do some research. The court reminds counsel that the LLC issue must be determined prior to the court ruling on a Petition for Final Distribution.

After additional discussion, the Accounting Petition and Status of Two-Year Accounting are continued to Jun-21-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The Order to Appear is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Two-Year Accounting
Off Calendar May-24-2022 Continued To Jun-21-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Apr-19-2022 Continued To May-24-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. An Order to Appear will be issued to Petitioner Wen Xu and Attorney Michael Spalding to appear on May-24-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why a Petition to Appoint a Guardian of the Estate has not been filed. The court is issuing an Order to Show Cause RE why the attorney should not be sanctioned $250, set for May-24-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court is ordering a two-year accounting from the date of death, two years forward and a Status of Accounting is set for May-24-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The hearing on the accounting petition is also continued to that date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-22-2022 Continued To Apr-19-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-28-2021 Continued To Mar-22-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Spalding appeared by zoom for Wen Xu.

The court stated that it has concerns regarding the minor, that it can't simply allow transfer of the property under the California Uniform Transfers to Minors Act without supervision and that a guardianship of the estate for the minor is needed in order to ensure that the property isn't sold or encumbered without court approval. The court also noted that the decedent appears to hold a one third interest in a 1.1 million dollar 720 6th Avenue property, and an interest in a Balboa Street property and a Wood Street property, and that there is no accounting for these properties/assets, or any status of the properties. Mr. Spalding referred to Probate Code 3906, and stated that nobody more than the mom holds the minor's best interest and that they are trying to avoid the expense of investigations and the reports. The First and Final Report of Administrator, etc. is continued to March 22, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for counsel to determine whether there is any income to be reported, and for establishment of a guardianship of the estate.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
